name: Linzer Squares & Thumbprints
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. For Squares: Line the bottom and 2 sides of an 8"x8"x2 inches metal baking pan with an 8"x16 inches strip of heavy duty aluminum foil, well buttered or greased.
3. For Cookies: Butter or grease 2 nonstick cookie sheets.
4. Place hazelnuts on a cookie sheet and bake them, stirring occasionally, for 10 to 12 minutes or until they turn golden where the skins crack. Cool to room temperature.
5. Food processor method:
- Cut butter into 1-inch cubes and wrap in plastic.
- Refrigerate.
6. In a food processor with metal blade, process nuts with half cup of flour until fine but not powder fine. Add remaining flour, sugar, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt. Process for a few seconds until evenly mixed. Pulse in butter until mixture has the consistency of fine crumbs. Add egg yolks, vanilla extract, and lemon zest. Pulse just until dough begins to hold together (do not allow it to form a ball).
7. Electric mixer method:
- Finely grate nuts.
- Soften butter.
- In a medium bowl, whisk flour, baking powder, cinnamon, salt, and lemon zest.
- Cream butter and sugar for about 3 minutes or until light and fluffy. Beat in egg yolks, then vanilla extract. Beat in flour mixture at low speed just until dough begins to hold together.
8. For the Squares: Remove one cup of dough and press into prepared pan. Spread 2/3 cup jam within 1/4 inches of edges. Set aside. Add three tablespoons egg whites to remaining dough and pulse or beat until incorporated. Scrape 1 cup dough into pastry bag, close securely, and pipe lattice on top of raspberry filling. Pipe first line down center, dividing square in half. Pipe additional lines dividing square into quarters and eighths. Turn pan and repeat process for total of 7 lines. Pipe border around edges.
9. Bake squares for 30 to 35 minutes or until golden brown and jam bubbles. Cool completely in pan on wire rack. Unmold by running spatula between sides and pastry on two sides without foil. Lift square with foil, turn, and cut into 16 2-inch squares.
10. For the cookies: Measure remaining dough into 1-1/4 inch scoop or 2 level teaspoons, roll to form 1-inch balls. If using nut coating, roll each ball in reserved egg whites, then chopped nuts. Place balls 1-1/2 inches apart on cookie sheets. Create depressions in center of each ball and fill with 1/4 teaspoon jam. Bake for 20 minutes or until lightly browned.
11. Store in airtight containers at room temperature or refrigerator. Keeps 1 week at room temperature, 2 weeks refrigerated. Squares keep several weeks refrigerated. Thumbprints become dry.
